mission.title: minio: STS AssumeRole - Getting SignatureDoesNotMatch with Presigned PutObject Request
mission.description: STS AssumeRole - Getting SignatureDoesNotMatch with Presigned PutObject Request. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify minio troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your minio deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n minio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio -n minio --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review minio configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant minio configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n minio -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio
kubectl get configmap -n minio -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=minio
```
I am trying to use AWS SDK's to allow untrusted clients temporary access to access files at specific prefixes in a bucket.

steps.title: Apply the fix for STS AssumeRole - Getting SignatureDoesNotMatch with…
steps.description:
fix: support pre-sign signature for STS tokens
```yaml
[REQUEST s3.PutObject] 23:53:59.968
PUT /bucket/file.jpg
Host: HOSTNAME.com
Content-Type: text/plain
Expect: 100-continue
X-Amz-Date: 20191011T235359Z
X-Amz-Security-Token: SESSION_TOKEN
Accept-Encoding: identity
Authorization: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=ACCESS_KEY/20191011/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=content-type;host;x-amz-content-sha256;x-amz-date;x-amz-security-token, Signature=7e73f94d9cf741652a0bd5cfa8589fc80393752cdaa4b18f436ca7c14a73391f
Content-Length: 0
User-Agent: aws-cli/1.16.113 Python/3.7.3 Linux/4.19.0-6-amd64 botocore/1.12.103
X-Amz-Content-Sha256: e3b0c44298fc1c1
```

steps.title: Confirm STS AssumeRole - Getting SignatureDoesNotMatch… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=minio -n minio --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n minio --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/minio/minio/pull/8826

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with minio installed or the issue environment reproducible.
